MINI MINUTES FOR MAY-03-2013 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MARIANA TAMBURO'S CLAIM EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART. BASED ON THE DEFENDANT'S FINANCIAL STATEMENTS, THE COURT ORDERS THAT HER WAGES BE WITHHELD IN THE AMOUNT OF $75 PER PAY PERIOD. THE ARITHMETIC ON THE FIRST PAGE OF DEFENDANT'S FINANCIAL STATEMENT IS INCORRECT. CORRECTLY CALCULATED BASED ON THE NUMBERS PROVIDED, SHE IN FACT SHOWS MONTHLY TAKE-HOME PAY OF $2724.23 (PRIOR TO WORKER'S COMP), WHICH EXCEEDS HER MONTHLY EXPENSES. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS DIRECTED TO RELEASE ANY EARNINGS HELD TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR FOR PAYMENT ON THE JUDGMENT. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. JUDGE: PETER BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185. *** RULING OF MAY 3, 2013 THAT GRANTED DEFENDANT'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IN PART IS VACATED PER EX PARTE ORDER OF MAY 8, 2013***

calendar.matters:
Hearing On Defendant'S Claim Of Exemption Per Ex Parte Order Of May 8, 2013 Vacating Ruling Of May 3, 2013 That Granted Defendant'S Claim Of Exemption In Part And (2) Recalendaring The Claim Of Exemption Hearing
Off Calendar Jun-13-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARIANA TIFFER, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER; No other appearances.

RULING: MATTER DISCUSSED. The Court VACATED its tentative ruling. THE COURT GRANTS DEFENDANT MARIANA TIFFER'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

Hearing On The Claim Exemption
Off Calendar May-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MARIANA TAMBURO, DEFENDANT IN PRO PER; No other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MARIANA TAMBURO'S Claim Exemption is granted in part. Based on the defendant's financial statements, the Court orders that her wages be withheld in the amount of $75 per pay period. The arithmetic on the first page of defendant's financial statement is incorrect. Correctly calculated based on the numbers provided, she in fact shows monthly take-home pay of $2724.23 (prior to worker's comp), which exceeds her monthly expenses. the levying officer is directed to release any earnings held to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment. ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING. Judge: Peter Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar May-03-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. PLAINTIFF CAPITAL ONE BANK (U.S.A.), N.A.'S HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is transferred to be heard on the law and motion calendar at 9:30 a.m. on the same date. Judge Pro Tem: Thomas A. Cohen; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
